phases.title: Foundation: App Shell, Navigation, Data Layer & Types
phases.description:
Create the entire app foundation. 

1. Write /frontend/pages/data/sampleData.ts — a comprehensive TypeScript data module exporting: demoRequests (typed array from the Demo tracker CSV with 50+ records including status: 'Reviewed'|'Canceled'|'Needs Review'|'DELETED'|'NEEDS REVIEW', geo: 'UK'|'US'|'JP'|'DE', type, requester, approver, organization (from guestsOrganization), host, date_requested, demo_date, start_time, end_time, vehicle_type, total_guests, total_vehicles, lead_days, readiness_date, slack_link, cancel_reason, channel, description), postDemoSessions (50+ records from Post Demo CSV with model, aux_model, vehicle_id, geo, demo_type, operator (submitter), route, weather, feature_rating, smoothness_rating, active_issues_list, uds_count, total_interventions, interventions object, model_behavior, driving_feature, comments, demo_datetime), operationFeedback (all records from Operation Feedback CSV with date, geo, operator, satisfaction_score, reported_issues, comments, feedback_host). Also export helper functions: normGeo(), normStatus(), leadBand(days: number) returning {key, label, color}, calcReadinessRate(), getDelayedOrders() (flags demos where lead_days < 3 as delayed). 

2. Write /frontend/pages/data/chartHelpers.ts — export aggregation helpers: aggregateDemosByMonth(), aggregateByGeo(), aggregateByType(), aggregateReadiness(), aggregateAvgSatisfaction(), aggregatePostDemoByModel(), aggregateInterventions(), aggregateIssues(). 

3. Write /frontend/components/AppShell.tsx — full sidebar layout. Left sidebar 240px wide (collapsible to 72px). Sidebar contains: brand logo area with 'Wayve Demo Ops' name, navigation items: Demo KPIs (/), Demo Tracker (/tracker), Demo Calendar (/calendar), Post Demo (/post-demo), Demo Cockpit (/cockpit), Demo Cost (/cost). Each nav item has icon (Lucide) + label. Active state highlighted in blue. Bottom: live sync status indicator (green dot + 'Live' text + last synced timestamp). Right side is scrollable content area. Sticky header bar inside content area with page title + filter controls. Use Tailwind CSS. Color scheme: background #F8FAFC, cards white, text #0F172A, muted #64748B, primary #2563EB. No dark mode needed but use semantic tokens where possible.

4. Write /frontend/components/StatCard.tsx — KPI card with accent color top border, large number value, label, optional sub-label, hover lift animation.

5. Write /frontend/components/DelayBadge.tsx — shows lead day band: 1-2 CRITICAL red #F20F31, 3-4 SHORT orange #F59E0B, 5-7 GOOD blue #0052FF, >7 EXCELLENT green #10B981, 0/null UNKNOWN gray.

6. Write /frontend/components/GeoBadge.tsx — colored geo badge: UK blue, JP red, US green, DE orange.

7. Write /frontend/components/StatusBadge.tsx — status chips: Reviewed green, Needs Review amber, Canceled red, DELETED gray.

8. Finally write /frontend/App.tsx with BrowserRouter Routes for all 6 pages wrapped in AppShell.
phases.notes: All foundation files created. Data at /frontend/pages/data/sampleData.ts (55 demoRequests, 55 postDemoSessions, 24 operationFeedback + helper fns) and /frontend/pages/data/chartHelpers.ts (8 aggregation helpers + aggregateGeoTrend bonus). Components: AppShell (collapsible 240/72px sidebar, Zap brand icon, 6 nav items, live sync footer), StatCard, DelayBadge, GeoBadge, StatusBadge. Stub pages created for all 6 routes. App.tsx wires routes inside AppShell with useLocation-based page titles. No dark mode on these components per spec.

phases.title: Demo KPIs Page: Executive Dashboard with Charts & Satisfaction Gauge
phases.description:
Build /frontend/pages/KPIPage.tsx — the executive overview dashboard.

Filters row at top: Month select (Jan-Dec + ALL), Geo select (ALL/UK/JP/US/DE), Type select (dynamically populated). All filters are state-controlled and filter all charts/tables on page.

Section 1 — KPI Cards row (6 cards): Total Demos, Total Guests, Approved Upcoming (demo_date >= today AND status=Reviewed), Cancelled count, Completion Rate (%), Avg Satisfaction Score (with 5 stars).

Section 2 — 3-column grid:
- Monthly Demo Trend: Recharts LineChart with gradient fill. Labels: month abbreviations. Show badge with total demo count.
- Geo Distribution: Recharts DoughnutChart 70% cutout. Colors UK=#3B82F6, JP=#EF4444, US=#22C55E, DE=#F59E0B. Custom legend alongside with counts.
- Demo Type Mix: DoughnutChart. VIP Demo=#AF52DE, External Guest=#3B82F6, Friends&Family=#22C55E, Candidate=#F59E0B, Internal=#22C55E, Recce/ST=#94A3B8.

Section 3 — 2-column grid:
- Pipeline Status: Stacked horizontal bar (Reviewed vs Needs Review vs Cancelled). Show completion rate at bottom.
- Geo Trend: Multi-line Recharts LineChart. One line per geo (UK/JP/US/DE). Last 12 months.

Section 4 — 3-column grid:
- Geo At A Glance: 4 colored mini-cards (UK blue, JP red, US green, DE orange) each showing geo name, count, progress bar.
- Demo Ops Satisfaction Gauge: Custom SVG semi-circle gauge. Use Recharts PieChart in semicircle mode (rotation:-90, circumference:180). 5 equal segments: red, orange, yellow, light-green, emerald. Needle drawn via Chart.js plugin equivalent using useRef canvas overlay. Center shows numeric score (e.g. 4.2). Render needle by drawing on a canvas overlay positioned absolutely above the Recharts chart.
- Readiness Status: Stacked bar with 4 buckets (CRITICAL/SHORT/GOOD/EXCELLENT) + readiness rate below.

Section 5 — Demo Feedback Details table (full width): Shows Date, Geo, Operator, Sat Score badge, Reported Issues, Comment, Host. Scrollable with sticky header. Data from operationFeedback filtered by current filters.

⚠️ DELAY FLAG: Add a prominent banner/alert at top of page if any demos have lead_days < 3 (or are past due date without being completed). Use AlertTriangle icon with amber background. Show count of flagged demos with 'View in Tracker' link.

Data: Import from /frontend/pages/data/sampleData and /frontend/pages/data/chartHelpers.
phases.notes: KPI page complete. 3 files: /frontend/pages/KPIPage.tsx (main), /frontend/pages/ui/SatisfactionGauge.tsx (semicircle gauge with canvas needle), /frontend/pages/ui/FeedbackTable.tsx (scrollable table). All 5 sections + delay banner + filter row implemented. Uses sampleData + chartHelpers. No new packages needed — all done with Recharts + canvas API.

phases.title: Demo Tracker Page: Kanban Board, Tables & Detail Drawer
phases.description:
Build /frontend/pages/TrackerPage.tsx — the operational tracking view.

Filters: same Month/Geo/Type selectors as KPI page. Also a search box for organization name.

⚠️ DELAY FLAG SECTION: At top, show a dismissible alert banner listing how many demos are delayed (lead_days < 3 and status not Canceled). Each flagged demo shown as a chip that scrolls to it.

Section 1 — Demo Pipeline Kanban Board:
Three columns: 'Needs Review' (amber header), 'Reviewed' (green header), 'Cancelled' (red header).
Each column has count badge and scrollable card list (max height 500px).
Kanban cards are compact agenda-style:
- Top row: organization name (bold) + geo badge (right)
- Bottom row: date (left) + READY badge if readiness_date exists + demo type (right)
- If slack_link: small '# Open in Slack' button
- If lead_days < 3: show DELAYED chip in red on card
Clicking a card opens a right-side drawer panel.

Detail Drawer (sheet from right, 460px wide):
- Status/Type/Geo badges row
- If delayed: red alert banner 'DELAYED — only X days lead time'
- When section
- Who section
- Where section  
- What section
- Links section (Slack anchor if exists)
- Lead Time bar (visual progress bar colored by band)
- 'Mark as Ready' button (sets readiness_date to today's date in state)
- If already ready: 'Ready — Marked DD-MM-YYYY' green chip

Section 2 — Readiness Table (collapsible):
Shows upcoming non-cancelled demos sorted by date.
Columns: Demo Date, Type, Organization, Geo, Host, Lead Days (with DelayBadge), Requester, Date of Readiness.
Rows with lead_days < 3 are highlighted with left red border (delayed flag).

Section 3 — Completed Demos Table (collapsible):
Past reviewed demos. Columns: Date, Type, Organization, Guests, Geo, Host, Lead Days, Requester, Date of Readiness.

Section 4 — Cancelled Table (collapsible):
Columns: Date, Geo, Type, Requester, Organization, Cancel Reason.

All tables use TanStack Table with sorting, sticky headers, and row hover states.

Delay logic: a demo is 'delayed' if lead_days is between 0-2 (inclusive) and status != Canceled. Show this consistently throughout the page with red indicators.
phases.notes: TrackerPage built across 3 files: /frontend/pages/TrackerPage.tsx (main page - filters, kanban, delay banner), /frontend/pages/ui/DemoDetailDrawer.tsx (Sheet drawer with lead-time bar + mark-ready), /frontend/pages/ui/TrackerTables.tsx (generic DataTable + 3 collapsible TanStack sections). Readiness overrides stored as Record<string,string> in TrackerPage state and passed down. isDelayed = lead_days < 3 && not Canceled/DELETED. Highlight-on-scroll implemented for delay banner chips. No new packages needed — uses existing @tanstack/react-table, shadcn Sheet/Collapsible/Table/Progress/Input/Select/Button, Lucide icons.

phases.title: Demo Calendar Page + Post Demo Analytics Page
phases.description:
BUILD TWO PAGES:

=== PAGE A: /frontend/pages/CalendarPage.tsx ===

Filters: Status, Geo, Month selects.

Layout: left calendar grid (70% width) + right agenda pane (30%).

Calendar grid:
- Weekday headers: Mon-Sun
- Days grid: each cell shows day number + up to 3 event chips
- Chip color: left border colored by status (green=Reviewed, orange=Needs Review, red=Cancelled)
- Chip text: 'GEO | ORG_NAME | TYPE_SHORT'
- Today highlighted with blue circle on date number
- Selected day has blue border
- Click day → shows agenda
- Month nav: prev/next arrows + month label + Today button
- Geo toggle buttons: UK/JP/US/DE flags

Agenda pane:
- Shows events for selected day sorted by start_time
- Each agenda card: type label, time, geo badge, org name, status chip, READY tag if readiness_date exists
- Clicking card opens same drawer as TrackerPage
- Pending (no date) section at bottom

Bottom legend: Approved (green dot), Needs Review (orange), Cancelled (red).

=== PAGE B: /frontend/pages/PostDemoPage.tsx ===

Filters: Month, Geo, Type selects.

KPI row (6 cards): Total Sessions, Total Interventions, Avg Int/Demo, Total Issues, Total UDs, Top Operator.

Top grid (3 columns):
- Geo Distribution donut
- Demo Type Mix donut
- Model Behavior Trends: Recharts AreaChart, x-axis=MM-DD dates, one area per top-5 behavior tag. Overlapped semi-transparent fills.

Row 2 (3 columns):
- Top Interventions: horizontal BarChart (indexAxis:y) showing top 8 intervention types by count. Red bars.
- Driving Feature Performance: vertical BarChart per feature. Multi-color bars.
- Vehicle Utilization: custom progress list (vehicle_id + session count + bar).

All Demo Sessions table (collapsible):
Columns: Date, Geo, Vehicle, Model, Issues (red bold if any, italic muted 'No issues' if none), Issue Count (number_of_uds, red if >0), Interventions (summary), Intervention Count, Operator.
Max 200 rows shown. TanStack Table with sorting.

Row 4 (50/50 grid):
- Left: Intervention Top Count table. Columns: #, Type, Count, Share bar. Red accent header.
- Right: Model Utilization horizontal bar chart (aggregated model+aux_model counts). Green accent (#10B981).

Row 5 (60/40 grid):
- Left: Operators Per Runs horizontal bar chart. Show count labels at bar end. Orange accent (#F59E0B).
- Right: Issue Breakdown horizontal bar chart (UI Crash, Power Cycle, Blue Light, UDs, GPS). Orange accent.
phases.notes:
CalendarPage at /frontend/pages/CalendarPage.tsx: filter row (Status select + Month select + Geo toggle flags), left 70% calendar grid with Mon-Sun headers, colored left-border event chips (green/orange/red by status), today=blue circle, selected day=blue ring, month prev/next nav, agenda pane (30%) showing selected day events sorted by start_time + pending review section (Needs Review demos this month), DemoDetailDrawer integration with readiness overrides.

PostDemoPage at /frontend/pages/PostDemoPage.tsx: Month/Geo/Type filter row, 6 KPI StatCards, Row1=Geo donut+Type donut+Behavior AreaChart (overlapping semi-transparent, top-5 categories), Row2=Top Interventions horizontal BarChart (red)+Feature Performance vertical BarChart (multi-color Cells)+Vehicle Utilization progress list, PostDemoSessions collapsible table (TanStack, 9 cols, sorting), Row4=Intervention Count table (red accent header + share bars)+Model Utilization horizontal bar (green), Row5=Operators Per Runs horizontal bar (orange, LabelList)+Issue Breakdown horizontal bar (orange).

PostDemoSessions at /frontend/pages/ui/PostDemoSessions.tsx: collapsible TanStack table, 9 columns (Date, Geo, Vehicle, Model, Issues red-bold/italic-muted, UDs red if >0, Interventions summary, Int Count, Operator), sticky header, alternating rows, sort by demo_datetime desc by default.

phases.title: Demo Cockpit Page + Demo Cost Page
phases.description:
BUILD TWO PAGES:

=== PAGE A: /frontend/pages/CockpitPage.tsx ===

Header: Action Required badge (count of Needs Review demos, red pill with pulsing dot).

Filters row:
- Geo segmented filter: All/UK/US/DE/JP
- Time segmented filter: All Time/This Week/Today/Tomorrow/Next Week

View switcher (4 buttons): Table, Board, List, Split.

Search bar: Status dropdown (All/Planned/Complete/Needs Review/Canceled) + org search input.

TABLE VIEW:
TanStack Table. Columns: Status badge, Schedule (date + time range), Organisation, Geo flag, Demo Type, Request Date, Actions (shortcuts row: Slack icon, Calendar icon, Reschedule, Approve, Cancel, Edit buttons).
Rows with lead_days < 3 have yellow left border (delayed highlight).
Approve button: changes status from Needs Review → Reviewed. Shows success toast.
Cancel button: opens dialog with reason selector (Client request, Vehicle unavailable, Driver unavailable, Route issue, Weather, Internal ops conflict, Safety concern, Other). On confirm: sets status to Canceled + stores reason.
Reschedule: opens dialog with date/time inputs. On confirm: updates demo_date, start_time, end_time in state.
Edit: opens expand modal with all editable fields.
Pagination: 15 rows per page.

BOARD VIEW:
4 columns: Needs Review, Planned (Reviewed+future), Complete (Reviewed+past), Canceled.
Kanban cards same style as TrackerPage.
Drag info text shown (no actual drag required, just visual).
Clicking card opens right side drawer with full detail.

LIST VIEW:
Accordion list. Each row: status badge + org name + date + type. Expanding shows full detail grid.

SPLIT VIEW (Mailbox):
Left panel 320px: scrollable card list (status + org + date + geo).
Right panel: full detail of selected record.

All mutations are optimistic (update local state immediately), show sonner toasts.

Delay indicator: any demo with lead_days 1-2 shows 'DELAYED' red badge in all views.

=== PAGE B: /frontend/pages/CostPage.tsx ===

Layout: left form card + right preview card.

Left — Cost Form:
Hero box (gradient blue) showing: 'Estimated Total Cost' label + large formatted cost + summary line.

Form fields:
- Estimate Name text input
- Region/Geo select (UK GBP / US USD / DE EUR)
- Main Demo Vehicles number input
- Add Backup Vehicle checkbox (reveals: Backup Distance km + Backup Work Hours)
- Distance (km per vehicle)
- Operators number
- Leads number
- Travel Hours (Total Duty Time)

Cost calculation logic (runs on every input change):
- Rates by geo: UK={opHr:23, leadHr:28, kmCost:0.14, hotel:125, meal:65}, US={opHr:30, leadHr:36, kmCost:0.16, hotel:200, meal:85}, DE={opHr:25, leadHr:30, kmCost:0.15, hotel:140, meal:70}
- totalDuty = travelH + 2
- teamSize = ops + leads
- needsHotel = travelH > 4 || totalDuty > 11 ? 1 : 0
- meals = needsHotel ? 2 : (totalDuty > 6 ? 1 : 0)
- staffCost = (ops * totalDuty * opHr) + (leads * totalDuty * leadHr)
- logCost = (teamSize * needsHotel * hotel) + (teamSize * meals * meal)
- fleetCost = vehicles * dist * kmCost
- backupCost = hasBackup ? (backupH * opHr + backupDist * kmCost) : 0
- grandTotal = staffCost + logCost + fleetCost + backupCost

Right — Preview card:
Cost Breakdown list (Staff, Logistics, Fleet, Backup — only non-zero rows).
Total row at bottom.

Below preview: Recent Estimates table (in-memory state array). Columns: Name, Geo, Total. Load/Delete buttons.
Save button: adds to in-memory estimates array with timestamp. Success toast.
phases.notes:
CockpitPage at /frontend/pages/CockpitPage.tsx: Action Required pulsing badge (count of NR demos), Geo segmented filter (All/UK/US/DE/JP), Time segmented filter (All Time/This Week/Today/Tomorrow/Next Week), View switcher (Table/Board/List/Split), Status+org search bar. TABLE VIEW: TanStack Table 15/page, yellow left border for lead_days<3, Status+DELAYED badge in col, all 6 action buttons (Slack/Calendar icons + Reschedule/Approve/Cancel/Edit). BOARD VIEW: 4 Kanban columns (Needs Review/Planned/Complete/Canceled), click→DemoDetailDrawer sheet. LIST VIEW: shadcn Accordion with full detail on expand. SPLIT VIEW: 320px scrollable card list + inline DetailPanel right side. All mutations optimistic with sonner toasts. CancelDialog/RescheduleDialog/EditDialog in /frontend/pages/ui/CockpitDialogs.tsx.

CostPage at /frontend/pages/CostPage.tsx: Gradient blue hero box showing live estimated total. Form fields: name, geo/currency select (UK/US/DE), vehicles, backup checkbox (reveals backupDist+backupH), dist, ops, leads, travelH. Live cost calc on every change using rate tables. Right preview: Staff/Logistics/Fleet/Backup breakdown + applied rates card + logistics trigger alert. Recent Estimates in-memory table with Load/Delete. Toaster added globally in App.tsx.

phases.title: Review the conversion for gaps and queue fix phases
phases.description:
Review the completed target app against the source classic app under
/context/linked-classic-app/. Identify conversion gaps, but do not fix them inline in
this phase.

Compare the source and target app coverage for:
- pages/routes
- containers and widgets
- queries, trigger behavior, and query parameters
- event handlers
- transformers, scripts, and state variables

For each material gap, append one focused fix phase with
`extend_phased_build_plan`. Each appended phase should be self-contained and small
enough for one context window.

Keep any comparison notes private or in tool arguments only; do not narrate the full
audit to the user. Do not re-queue gaps already covered by existing phases.

When all identified gaps have been queued, call `complete_build_phase`. If no gaps
are found, call `complete_build_phase` without appending phases.
phases.notes: Source classic app was empty (single Page 1, no widgets, no queries, empty Frame). No conversion gaps exist — target app is a complete greenfield build.
